author: nflaig
content:
I do prefer this on the beacon node as well, but I really like to avoid introducing another endpoint which cannot support ssz due to using an enum

<@680234424037670912> based on your comment here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/710831784991916072/1505695972045619200, you rather have it resolved by client, I think that was what Jim's v2 validators api proposal did?

author: nflaig
content: ah yes the whole point there was to remove `status`

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/449

author: nflaig
content: builder endpoints (#610)

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/601 is not something we wanna consider? I feel like in general it would be nice to have an enum represenation in ssz so we are not blocked by such simple issues.. a long time ago I was thinking about adding `Enum` to the ssz spec, maybe should revive that effort

author: .paulharris
content:
i just don't think we need to be creating meta objects and v2 did a better job, but it depends on the data ppl are actually using which we have no metrics on.
Generating 1GB of data from a BN is a sure-fire way to nuke it, i certainly dont think we should be encouraging fetching a 2 million item list in any format, but if we do have to then the standard validator structure is 'less bad' than doing computation to create a new meta object for 2 million entries.

My preference if we're generating a meta object is to be severely restricting the scope and not allowing open ended responses of the entire data set

author: .paulharris
content: 100% i think we need ssz support (imo for all endpoints)

author: .paulharris
content: worst case, i think /validators being able to return the entire ssz straight off the state is 'less bad' if ppl really want the whole data set

author: .paulharris
content: also i haven't measured the performance of this query since we were around 400k so im not sure how long the response would take now

author: nflaig
content: agree, would a generic enum ssz type be something we should consider? it seems like a gap in ssz to be a proper wire format

author: .paulharris
content: a translation to enum im not strictly against, but the use case im dubious of given the sheer size of the data set

author: .paulharris
content: its arguably less expensive to return a number

author: .paulharris
content: does mean any consumer needs to be understanding that value, and also arguably is a breaking change

author: .paulharris
content: if it was just ssz and we continue to return string in json, then its not breaking

author: .paulharris
content: horrible to implement :/ but non breaking

author: nflaig
content: what you mean by number?

author: .paulharris
content: enum is a number...

author: .paulharris
content: if we broke the json it'd save MB

author: nflaig
content: it would just be a byte mapping, we just need a simple way to define that, ideally something generic but #601 would work to and shouldn't be breaking as it just affects ssz which we currently don't support

author: .paulharris
content:
if we're going to do this whole change to support ssz then its non trivial. id really like to understand why ppl are consuming it and if they really need all the data we're returning if we go down this road.
given thats not going to happen though, we can add ssz support and clients can choose to implement it? i just dont like the shape of this endpoint at all

author: .paulharris
content: its actually tempting to deprecate it and build what ppl need, but we dont know what ppl need ultimately

author: nflaig
content:
so the pain is just how we wanna deal with `status` which was the main blocker on https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/449 as we didn't wanna drop it and same point raised by Potuz about builder endpoints here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/710831784991916072/1509176030386983182, also I remember some discussion with rocketpool that they would prefer to have the `status` in the response

for builders it is simpler, as you could just compute it on the client side by fetching the finalized checkpoint

author: .paulharris
content: for example, if just status of specific validators is needed, then thats a very different shape to a complete exhaustive dump of validators

author: .paulharris
content: its a lot of data to fetch if you're just looking for status

author: nflaig
content:
from validator client point of view, we want the whole validator (or at least index) + status, ideally ssz encoded, the validator identities endpoint solves the minimal use case

but not sure how I feel about introducing that whole set of endpoints for builders too, we could have an endpoint that just returns statuses but that seems ugly as well

author: .paulharris
content: im not against the builder endpoints as a theory as long as we're not introducing tech debt like the get+post etc

author: nflaig
content: yeah that for sure not, but what about the custom object in Potuz's Pr with the `status`?

author: nflaig
content: also should not need a separate endpoint for balances, left another comment here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/610#discussion_r3314209359

author: .paulharris
content: i need to look up how builder balances worked to answer that properly, i assumed they had effective and actual balances

author: .paulharris
content: if they dont then sure - i defer to your judgement on that one

author: .paulharris
content: at least by the state object shape it looks like a separate balance endpoint would not be required

author: .paulharris
content: not sure if we'd need to have access to pending payments and withdrawals?

author: .paulharris
content:
ok - so 
 - ssz support
 - implies support for status as enum
 - balance is built into builder object anyway
 - status is presumably a new set of rules, they have differing requirements compared to validators? withdrawal etc all dift
